channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: California’s new AI deepfakes law gets blocked by a federal judge. Here’s why
item.description: A new California law allowing any person to sue for damages over election deepfakes has been put on pause after a federal judge granted a preliminary injunction Wednesday blocking it. U.S. District Judge John A.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Russian Intelligence-Linked Hacking Group Targeted Western Think Tanks, Journalists: Microsoft
item.description: Microsoft and U.S. authorities said a hacking group tied to Russian intelligence tried to infiltrate systems of Western think tanks, journalists, and former military officials.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: JNUC 2024: Jamf brings AI and more to Apple enterprise
item.description: Apple management and security services provider Jamf unveiled a series of tools and features to support Apple admins at its well-attended JNUC event Oct. 1, with a focus on AI, security, and all-new solutions to make it easier to manage large Apple fleets.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Jersey offshore wind farm clears big federal hurdle amid environmental concerns
item.description: SEA GIRT, N.J. (AP) — The federal government gave a key approval Tuesday to an offshore wind farm in New Jersey, even as residents in the town where its power cable would come ashore worry it could go through underground toxic waste that's still being cleaned up. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Swiss glaciers are receding again after 2 punishing years and despite a good start to 2024
item.description: Experts say the volume of Switzerland’s glaciers shrank again this summer and compounded the negative impact of climate change after a devastating two-year run that depleted the ice by more than 10%.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: JRuby 10 due to arrive in early-2025
item.description: JRuby, which dates back to 2001 as a Ruby language implementation for the JVM, is set to arrive at version 10 shortly after the new year. JRuby 10 promises to be fully compatible with Ruby 3.4.0, a planned update to Ruby that brings changes for frozen string literals and class updates.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Verizon says service has 'returned to normal levels' after thousands experienced outages
item.description: Verizon said Monday its "engineers have fully restored today's network disruption" following thousands of its users reporting outages. "Verizon engineers have fully restored today's network disruption that impacted some customers.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Arkansas sues YouTube over claims that the site is fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Arkansas sued YouTube and parent company Alphabet on Monday, saying the video-sharing platform is made deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth in the state.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cyber pay in government is as fragmented as ever
item.description: It’s a case of “haves and have-nots.” Here’s an informal run down of agencies with special cyber pay authorities, including DoD, DHS, and the VA. The post Cyber pay in government is as fragmented as ever first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump says he's spoken to Musk about getting Starlink for areas damaged by Helene
item.description: Former President Trump on Monday said he’s spoken with Elon Musk about providing Starlink internet service for those without power in states ravaged by Hurricane Helene. Trump visited Valdosta, Ga., where he toured storm damage and expressed support for those dealing with the aftermath.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Intel launches Xeon 6 processors and Gaudi 3 AI accelerators
item.description: Intel has formally launched its next Xeon 6 server processors as well as the Gaudi 3 AI accelerators, making some pretty big boasts in the process.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fortnite maker accuses Samsung, Google of colluding to block app store competitors
item.description: Fortnite maker Epic Games filed a lawsuit Monday accusing Google and Samsung, the world’s largest Android maker, of conspiring to block third-party competitors of the Google Play Store.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Intel’s Altera spinout launches FPGA products, software
item.description: Altera – Intel’s standalone company focused on FPGA hardware – introduced an array of FPGA hardware, software, and services at its annual developer conference.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to succeed with Kubernetes
item.description: Kubernetes, the open source orchestration platform for automating the deployment and management of containerized workloads and services, continues to gain popularity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Chinese EV tech ban not an overreaction: National security experts
item.description: A tech ban on EVs will limit the Chinese government from accessing data on American drivers, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Zero Trust + AI: A match made in the clouds
item.description: Have any terms in the cybersecurity space generated more buzz than “zero trust” and “AI”? At the risk of sounding like a Magic 8 Ball, my reply is no.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Kristen Bell is a Meta AI voice after criticizing company in post
item.description: After posting an "opt out" declaration on Instagram, the actress has now signed with Meta to use her voice in its AI chatbot.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: X requests it be reinstated in Brazil after complying with judge's orders, sources say
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — In the high-stakes showdown between the world’s richest man and a Brazilian Supreme Court justice, Elon Musk blinked. Musk’s social media site X has complied with Alexandre de Moraes’ orders and requested its service be reestablished in the country, two sources said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Journalist booted from X after publishing hacked Vance document
item.description: Klippenstein published the 271-page report compiled by the Trump campaign to vet Vance on his Substack.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation
